prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_audit_entity producer_component_p1)
(allocate_log_channel_to_entity producer_component_p1 log_channel_a)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(authorize_entity_for_collection producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(release_event_schema_from_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(register_audit_entity consumer_component_c1)
(allocate_log_channel_to_entity consumer_component_c1 log_channel_b)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1)
(authorize_entity_for_collection consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1)
(bind_operator_identity_to_entity consumer_component_c1 operator_identity_ops)
(prepare_consumer_stream_for_collection consumer_component_c1 consumer_stream_s1 event_schema_v1)
(lock_consumer_partition_for_composition consumer_component_c1 consumer_stream_s1 operator_identity_ops)
(release_operator_identity_from_entity consumer_component_c1 operator_identity_ops)
(release_event_schema_from_entity consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1)
(attach_evidence_to_producer_partition producer_component_p1 producer_stream_s1 evidence_resource_r1)
(compose_audit_artifact_request_encryption producer_component_p1 consumer_component_c1 producer_stream_s1 consumer_stream_s1 audit_artifact_a1)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(ingest_producer_evidence_into_stream producer_component_p1 producer_stream_s1 event_schema_v1 evidence_resource_r1)
(seal_audit_artifact audit_artifact_a1 producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(finalize_producer_component producer_component_p1 audit_artifact_a1)
(finalize_consumer_component consumer_component_c1 audit_artifact_a1)
(release_event_schema_from_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(attach_credential_to_entity producer_component_p1 credential_creds1 event_schema_v1)
(finalize_producer_with_credential producer_component_p1 log_channel_a credential_creds1)
(release_event_schema_from_entity producer_component_p1 event_schema_v1)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1)
(attach_credential_to_entity consumer_component_c1 credential_creds1 event_schema_v1)
(finalize_consumer_with_credential consumer_component_c1 log_channel_b credential_creds1)
(release_event_schema_from_entity consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1)
(register_audit_entity audit_coordinator_alpha)
(allocate_log_channel_to_entity audit_coordinator_alpha log_channel_c)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity audit_coordinator_alpha event_schema_v1)
(authorize_entity_for_collection audit_coordinator_alpha event_schema_v1)
(attach_policy_template_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha policy_template_p1)
(bind_operator_identity_to_entity audit_coordinator_alpha operator_identity_ops)
(attach_encryption_key_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha encryption_key_k1)
(attach_timestamp_token_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha timestamp_token_t1)
(ingest_evidence_fragment_into_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha evidence_fragment_f1 audit_artifact_a1)
(activate_policy_on_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha evidence_fragment_f1 audit_artifact_a1 event_schema_v1 policy_template_p1)
(apply_encryption_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha encryption_key_k1 operator_identity_ops evidence_fragment_f1 audit_artifact_a1)
(release_encryption_key_from_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha encryption_key_k1)
(apply_timestamp_and_record_policy_check audit_coordinator_alpha timestamp_token_t1 evidence_fragment_f1 audit_artifact_a1)
(release_timestamp_token_from_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha timestamp_token_t1)
(attach_compliance_check_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha compliance_check_c1)
(execute_compliance_workflow_on_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha producer_component_p1 consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1 compliance_check_c1)
(finalize_and_publish_coordinator audit_coordinator_alpha)
(attach_credential_to_entity audit_coordinator_alpha credential_creds1 event_schema_v1)
(finalize_coordinator_with_credential audit_coordinator_alpha log_channel_c credential_creds1)
(release_event_schema_from_entity audit_coordinator_alpha event_schema_v1)
(release_operator_identity_from_entity audit_coordinator_alpha operator_identity_ops)
(register_audit_entity audit_coordinator_beta)
(allocate_log_channel_to_entity audit_coordinator_beta log_channel_a)
(bind_event_schema_to_entity audit_coordinator_beta event_schema_v1)
(authorize_entity_for_collection audit_coordinator_beta event_schema_v1)
(attach_policy_template_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta policy_template_p2)
(bind_operator_identity_to_entity audit_coordinator_beta operator_identity_ops)
(attach_encryption_key_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta encryption_key_k1)
(attach_timestamp_token_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta timestamp_token_t1)
(ingest_evidence_fragment_into_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta evidence_fragment_f2 audit_artifact_a1)
(activate_policy_on_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta evidence_fragment_f2 audit_artifact_a1 event_schema_v1 policy_template_p2)
(apply_encryption_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta encryption_key_k1 operator_identity_ops evidence_fragment_f2 audit_artifact_a1)
(release_encryption_key_from_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta encryption_key_k1)
(apply_timestamp_and_record_policy_check audit_coordinator_beta timestamp_token_t1 evidence_fragment_f2 audit_artifact_a1)
(release_timestamp_token_from_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta timestamp_token_t1)
(attach_compliance_check_to_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta compliance_check_c2)
(execute_compliance_workflow_on_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta producer_component_p1 consumer_component_c1 event_schema_v1 compliance_check_c2)
(finalize_and_publish_coordinator audit_coordinator_beta)
(attach_credential_to_entity audit_coordinator_beta credential_creds1 event_schema_v1)
(finalize_coordinator_with_credential audit_coordinator_beta log_channel_a credential_creds1)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a complex audit traceability design domain with many actions and predicates. The goal is to finalize four entities: producer_component_p1, consumer_component_c1, audit_coordinator_alpha, and audit_coordinator_beta.


## Examining Entity Design  
- The task focuses on finalizing four entities in a complex audit traceability system.  
- Entities include producer_component_p1, consumer_component_c1, audit_coordinator_alpha, and audit_coordinator_beta.  
- The domain involves many actions and predicates, suggesting a detailed structure.  
- The goal is to ensure each entity's role is clearly defined for audit purposes.

## Examining Entity Design  
- All entities—producer_component_p1, consumer_component_c1, audit_coordinator_alpha, and audit_coordinator_beta—need initial registration, as none are registered yet.  
- Channels (a, b, c) are limited to three, but four entities require allocation, suggesting sequential or shared use during finalization.  
- Some elements like streams and coordinators are pre-bound, simplifying certain steps like producer_component_p1 to producer_stream_s1 and consumer_component_c1 to consumer_stream_s1.  
- Entities treated as logical_partitions (producer and consumer) must follow the same finalization process, including channel and credential binding.  

## Checking Initial Conditions  
- Key resources available include event_schema_v1, operator, evidence_resource_r1, and timestamp_token_t1, with evidence fragments f1 and f2 assigned to coordinators alpha and beta.  
- Audit_artifact_a1 is pending composition, while policies (p1, p2), compliance (c1, c2), credential creds1, and encryption k1 are also available.  
- External reference xref1 is available, but xref2 is bound yet not initially available, impacting coordinator bindings.  
- Resources like schemas and operators can be bound and released, allowing reuse during the process.  

## Planning Finalization Steps  
- Finalization requires registration, channel allocation, schema binding, operator binding, credential binding, and eventual finalization for all entities.  
- Producers and consumers, along with coordinators, need to be credential_bound and finalized with credentials, ensuring eligibility checks are met.  
- The process likely involves finalizing entities sequentially due to limited channels, releasing them for reuse as each entity completes.  
- Goal is to ensure all four entities reach entity_finalized status, balancing resource constraints like the single evidence resource.

## Analyzing Resource Constraints  
- Only one evidence_resource r1 is available, limiting simultaneous evidence use for producer and consumer, likely requiring sequential processing.  
- Shared resources like encryption k1, timestamp t1, and credential creds1 suggest entities may need to be finalized one at a time to manage constraints.  

## Evaluating Compose Variants  
- Compose for request_encryption needs producer_stream_evidence_locked and consumer_stream_ready, allowing ingestion afterward to set ready_comp.  
- Different compose variants (e.g., request_timestamp, both) dictate pre-ingestion conditions, affecting when entities can proceed to finalization.  

## Assessing Coordinator Dependencies  
- Coordinators alpha and beta share artifact a1, p1, and c1, but beta’s binding to unavailable xref2 may delay some actions, like attach_external_reference.  
- Actions like prepare_coordinator_with_fragment depend on artifact composition and sealing, requiring careful sequencing with other entity finalization steps.

## Sequencing Entity Preparation  
- Registration and channel allocation for p1 enable schema binding, consuming available resources like schema v1 and operator ops.  
- Preparing producer stream p1 requires bound schema for prepare_stream, but lock_producer_partition only needs prepared entity and bound operator, not schema.  

## Managing Schema Constraints  
- Schema must remain bound for seal action, as seal_producer requires bound_schema, preventing release before sealing.  
- Releasing schema after locking is impossible without affecting seal, requiring sequential binding to p1 and c1 for their respective preparations.  

## Exploring Evidence Path  
- Evidence path also requires bound_schema for ingest_producer_evidence, reinforcing the need for schema availability at multiple stages.  
- Composing requires both streams locked and ready_comp, necessitating schema binding for both p1 and c1 before composition can proceed.  

## Planning Resource Reuse  
- To share the single schema, bind to p1, complete prepare_stream and lock, then release for c1 to repeat the process, ensuring both reach ready_comp.  
- Finalization steps like seal and finalize_producer_with_credential suggest schema can be unbound after compose, but must be managed carefully for symmetric consumer steps.

## Sequencing Entity Steps
- Register and allocate channels for both producer p1 and consumer c1 before binding schema v1 to prepare each entity.
- Authorize p1 and c1 with bound schema to set them as prepared, then release schema from p1 to make it available for c1.

## Managing Schema Release
- After preparing consumer c1 and locking its partition, release schema from c1 to free it for producer p1’s later steps.
- Ensure schema is rebound to p1 for ingest_producer_evidence, as it requires entity_bound_to_schema for completion.

## Validating Compose Conditions
- Compose_audit_artifact_request_encryption requires both p1 and c1 locked, streams bound, and readiness conditions met for both entities.
- Post-compose, producer p1 needs additional evidence ingestion to achieve ready_comp, ensuring proper sequencing.

## Reviewing Producer Needs  
- Producer doesn't require bound operator for attaching evidence, only needing prepared state, bound stream, and available evidence.  
- Finalizing producer also doesn't need operator, requiring only locked state, ready composition, and sealed artifact.  

## Managing Consumer Operations  
- Consumer requires binding operator for locking partition, as lock_consumer_partition needs bound_op.  
- Operator can be released after locking, freeing it for reuse in other entities.  

## Preparing Coordinator Steps  
- Coordinator needs to ingest evidence fragments without requiring schema, focusing on prepared state and artifact binding.  
- Policy enforcement on coordinator requires attaching policy template first, without needing schema at that stage.  

## Handling Encryption Requirements  
- Applying encryption to coordinator requires prior binding of encryption key, ensuring prepared state and key availability.  
- Policy enforcement must be started before applying encryption, aligning with artifact encryption requests.

## Finalizing Coordinator Status  
- Coordinator achieves ready_for_finalization status after policy passes, compliance is bound, and schema remains bound, marking it eligible for final steps.  
- Finalizing and publishing the coordinator commits it, making it eligible for credential binding without requiring explicit policy checks.  

## Planning Credential Attachment  
- Attaching credentials requires the coordinator to be eligible, schema bound, and credentials available, ensuring proper sequencing.  
- Finalizing with credentials releases both the channel and credential, making them available for subsequent coordinators.  

## Managing Resource Reuse  
- Encryption keys and timestamps remain bound after finalization, requiring explicit release for reuse in other coordinators.  
- Release actions, like release_encryption_key_from_coordinator, are needed post-application to free resources for subsequent steps.  

## Coordinating Multiple Entities  
- Plan the order of operations to manage shared resources like schema and credentials, ensuring one coordinator is fully finalized before starting another.  
- Operator and channel bindings need careful release to avoid conflicts, especially when working with multiple coordinators like alpha and beta.

## Sequencing Operations  
- The correct order is to attach evidence to the producer partition first, then compose the audit artifact request encryption, ensuring evidence is locked and not ready for composition.  
- After composing, ingest evidence into the stream, which sets the producer ready for completion, requiring the schema to be bound beforehand.  

## Managing Schema Binding  
- Binding the event schema to the producer component is necessary before ingesting evidence, ensuring stream readiness.  
- Schema can be released after finalization but needs to be rebound for credential attachment, maintaining eligibility for further steps.  

## Finalizing Components  
- Finalizing the producer and consumer components requires them to be eligible, achieved after sealing the audit artifact and ensuring schema binding.  
- Attaching credentials to finalize involves binding the schema again, ensuring the component is ready for credential attachment and finalization.

## Managing Compliance Checks  
- Attaching compliance checks to coordinators like audit_coordinator_alpha involves binding compliance_check_c1, making it unavailable for others.  
- Executing compliance workflows requires all components (producer, consumer, schema) to be ready, ensuring the coordinator is set for finalization.

## Finalizing with Credentials  
- Attaching credentials to coordinators requires the schema to be bound, finalizing with credentials makes both the channel and credentials available again.  
- The process repeats for other coordinators, like audit_coordinator_beta, ensuring resources are reused efficiently.

## Releasing Resources  
- Encryption keys and timestamp tokens remain bound after use, requiring explicit release (e.g., release_encryption_key_from_coordinator) for reuse in subsequent coordinators.  
- Operations and schema bound to one coordinator can be released after finalization, preparing resources for the next coordinator like beta.

## Reviewing Coordinator Finalization  
- Finalizing audit_coordinator_beta with credential_creds1 and event_schema_v1 completes the process, making log_channel_a available afterward.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
